question_html:
Write down the meanings of the following idioms/phrases and use each of them in a sentence of your own.

Break the ice, A piece of cake, Hit the nail on the head, Once in a blue moon, Burn the midnight oil.
solution_html:
Break the ice

Meaning: To start a conversation in a social setting and make people feel more comfortable.

Sentence: To break the ice, the teacher told a funny story on the first day of class.

A piece of cake

Meaning: Something very easy to do.

Sentence: The math test was a piece of cake for Rafi.

Hit the nail on the head

Meaning: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem.

Sentence: When she said we need more practice, she hit the nail on the head.

Once in a blue moon

Meaning: Very rarely.

Sentence: I eat ice cream only once in a blue moon.

Burn the midnight oil

Meaning: To study or work late into the night.

Sentence: He had to burn the midnight oil to finish his assignment.

Marks Distribution:

(0.25 × 5) = 1.25 marks for writing the meanings of the idioms/phrases

(0.25 × 5) = 1.25 marks for making meaningful sentences with idioms/phrases.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes and 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.]

question_html: “All human beings are born free and equal.”-explain the statement shortly in your own words.
solution_html:
“All human beings are born free and equal” is a part of article-1 of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.

The statement means that every person has inherent worth and the same basic rights from birth. It signifies that no individual is naturally superior or inferior to another. This principle is central to democratic values and human rights laws worldwide. It advocates for fair treatment and opportunities for all people, regardless of race, gender, nationality, or other characteristics. The idea underscores the importance of freedom and equality in creating just societies. It opposes discrimination and oppression in any form. This principle inspires efforts to protect and promote human rights universally.

Marks Distribution:

01 mark for writing the Core Meaning & giving Explanation of Equality.

01 mark for writing the Context & significance

0.5 mark for a conclusion statement.

[N.B: 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two (spelling or grammatical) mistakes and 01 mark for three or more (spelling or grammatical) mistakes.]
